codeReview.summary: The implementation correctly provides test prompts across all 4 required domains (code, JSON, conversational, repetitive) with 5 prompts per category (20 total). The TrainTestSplitter implements the 60%/40% split methodology correctly. Tokenizer integration is provided via the TokenizerAdapter class with character-level fallback. The code is well-structured with dataclasses, enums, and type hints. Documentation is thorough with clear usage examples.

issues.description: TrainTestSplitter.split_text() splits on character boundaries, not token boundaries. For n-gram cache building at the token level, token-aware splitting would be more accurate. However, the split() method supports token-level splitting when a tokenizer is provided, so this is acceptable.
Complex inline import statement for PromptCategory (getattr(__import__(...))). Works correctly but could be simplified by importing at the top of the function.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The speculative-validation-worker skill says 'Use existing test prompts from tests/val_m6_speculative.py' but the worker created a new dedicated prompt module. This is an improvement over the hardcoded prompts, but the skill file could be updated to reference the new module.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill file at .factory/skills/speculative-validation-worker/SKILL.md line 17 says 'Use existing test prompts from tests/val_m6_speculative.py'. The worker created src/inference/prompts.py and updated val_m6_speculative.py to import from it.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The PROMPTS_README.md documents expected n-gram acceptance rates by category (Code: 50-60%, JSON: 45-55%, Conversational: 40-50%, Repetitive: 70-80%). This is useful context for validation that could be added to the mission's AGENTS.md or the speculative-decode.md library file.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: data/PROMPTS_README.md includes a 'Performance Expectations' section with estimated acceptance rates by domain.

summary: Feature m2-prepare-real-text-prompts is complete and functional. The prompt module provides 20 test prompts across 4 domains with proper train/test split methodology (60%/40%). All verification checks pass. Minor non-blocking issues noted around character vs token-level splitting. Integration with val_m6_speculative.py is clean with fallback to hardcoded prompts for robustness.
